logline: A literary translator realizes the window for her craft is closing as machine translation captures the nuances she once thought untranslatable.
scene: a literary translator at a wooden desk covered in manuscript pages and open books in two languages, warm desk lamp casting golden light, a laptop showing side-by-side text columns, her pen hovering over a page with sparse red annotations, rain visible through a window behind, Bergen Norway atmosphere
keyProps: Manuscript pages and open books in two languages
Warm desk lamp casting golden light
A pen hovering over sparse red annotations
camera: Eye-level workspace framing, 50mm lens with shallow depth of field, intimate documentary style
lighting: Warm golden desk lamp light with cool rain-filtered daylight from the window behind
productionDesign: Documentary realism with warm golden palette, subtle film grain, cozy Bergen Norway study filled with books and manuscripts
signatureTwist: close-up of two open books side by side on a weathered wooden desk, one in Norwegian and one in English, a human hand resting between them with a pen that has barely been used, warm lamplight casting shadows across the pages, a glass of aquavit catching the light, shallow depth of field, the small gap between the books as visual metaphor
